# UC-13.7.8 · Fluent Bit Storage Backlog File Count and Age

## Description

Persistent chunk files that do not drain indicate the output is slower than ingress for extended periods—often TLS issues, HEC 503s, or disk IO limits on IoT gateways. Count and age together separate brief spikes from stuck backlogs.

## Value

Prevents storage exhaustion on edge devices and highlights regional Splunk indexer saturation.

## Implementation

Align `input_name`/`output_name` with Fluent Bit config. Join to host disk free metrics. Alert when `age` exceeds RPO targets for that log class.

## SPL

```spl
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentbit:storage"
| eval chunks_up=coalesce(chunks_up, up_chunks, storage_chunks_up)
| eval max_age_sec=coalesce(chunk_up_age_sec, oldest_chunk_age_sec)
| where chunks_up > 50 OR max_age_sec > 600
| stats latest(chunks_up) as up, latest(max_age_sec) as age by host, input_name, output_name
| sort -age
```

## Visualization

Dual-axis (chunks × age), map of edge sites, table sorted by age.

## References

- [Fluent Bit — Monitoring](https://docs.fluentbit.io/manual/administration/monitoring)
- [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
